# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKV } from "https://deno.land/std@0.140.0/denops/mod.ts";
import { KVNamespace } from "https://deno.land/x/kv@1.2.3/mod.ts";

const kv = await openKV("edge_database");

async function searchUsers(req: Request) {
  const userId = req.params.get("user_id");
  if (!userId) return new Response("User ID is required", { status: 400 });

  const filterParam = req.url.searchParams.get("filter");
  if (filterParam && !["name", "email"].includes(filterParam)) {
    return new Response(`Invalid filter parameter: ${filterParam}`, { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const key = ["users", userId, filterParam].join("_");
    const value = await kv.get(key);
    if (value) {
      return new Response(value, { headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } });
    } else {
      return new Response("No results found", { status: 404 });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response(`Error searching users: ${error}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}

addEventListener("fetch", async (event: FetchEvent) => {
  const req = event.request;
  const res = await searchUsers(req);
  event.respondWith(res);
});
